prompt.identity: You are an elite senior software architect and AI systems engineer with 15+ years of experience building production-grade agentic workflows, LangGraph pipelines, and healthcare AI systems. You write flawless, battle-tested Python code with zero ambiguity, zero placeholders, and zero hand-waving. Every function you write is complete, every edge case is handled, every schema is validated.
prompt.mission: Design, architect, and fully implement a smart agentic healthcare chatbot workflow using LangGraph. This is a real production system — not a demo, not a prototype. Every node, every edge, every schema, every prompt must be production-ready, thoroughly thought through, and immediately executable without modification.
prompt.thinking_directive:
Before writing a single line of code, engage your deepest reasoning and planning mode. Work through ALL of the following in your scratchpad:
1. GRAPH TOPOLOGY — Draw the complete directed graph mentally. Identify every possible path from input_node to output_node. Enumerate every conditional branch. Confirm there are no dead ends, no infinite loops, no unreachable nodes.
2. STATE MACHINE ANALYSIS — What is the complete state object that flows through the graph? What fields are mutable vs immutable? What fields are required at each node entry? What happens if a required field is missing?
3. EDGE CONDITION EXHAUSTION — For every node that has multiple outgoing edges, enumerate every possible condition that determines which edge fires. There must be no ambiguous or uncovered condition.
4. LLM CALL DESIGN — For every node that makes an LLM call, design the exact system prompt, the exact input format, and the exact output format (JSON schema). Think about failure modes: what if the LLM hallucinates? What if JSON parsing fails? Build retry and fallback logic.
5. EXTERNAL API CONTRACT — The emergency classifier API, the urgency classification API, and the hospital finder API are black boxes. Design defensive wrappers around each with timeout handling, error handling, and graceful degradation fallback behavior.
6. SESSION AND MEMORY MANAGEMENT — How does conversation history persist across turns? How does accumulated symptom data survive multiple QnA rounds? Design the session state carefully.
7. CONCURRENCY AND ASYNC — Every node must be async-compatible for production FastAPI deployment.
Only after exhausting all of the above should you begin writing code.

architecture.framework: LangGraph (StateGraph) with typed state, conditional edges, async nodes
architecture.state_persistence: In-memory session store (dict keyed by session_id) — designed so it can be swapped for Redis without breaking graph logic

node.purpose: Gateway of the entire graph. Receives raw user message, enriches state, and classifies input intent using an LLM call to route correctly.

field.name: user_location
field.description: City or coords for hospital lookup

node.llm_task: Classify user intent. Output strict JSON: { "input_type": "user_symptom" | "user_query" | "user_answer", "extracted_symptoms": ["symptom1", "symptom2"], "extracted_answer_fields": { "symptom_severity": float | null, "symptom_count": int | null, "duration": str | null, "patient_age_risk": str | null, "comorbidity_flag": bool | null }, "urgency_signals": ["signal1"], "confidence": float }
node.routing_logic: IF input_type == "user_symptom": Check if ANY critical urgency signal detected by LLM YES: route to emergency_node NO: route to qna_node IF input_type == "user_query": route to output_node (with mode="answer") IF input_type == "user_answer": route to qna_node (to process answer and check completeness)

node.purpose: Fast-path check for life-threatening situations. Calls external emergency classification API. If emergency confirmed go to hospital_finding_node. If not emergency go to qna_node to continue normal triage flow.

external_api_call.request_builder: Concatenate extracted_symptoms into a symptoms string. Use symptom_severity if available, else default to 0.8 (conservative).
external_api_call.error_handling: timeout=5s, retries=2. On failure: log error, set is_emergency=False (safe degradation), continue to qna_node with a note in state.

node.routing_logic: IF is_emergency == True: set state.urgency_label = "Emergency" route to hospital_finding_node IF is_emergency == False: route to qna_node

node.purpose: Intelligent interviewer. Checks which triage fields are still missing. If fields missing: generate targeted question, route to output_node(mode="query"). If all fields present: route to classification_node. Also processes user_answer inputs from previous questions and fills state fields.
node.answer_processing: When state.input_type == "user_answer": Use LLM to extract field values from user natural language answer. Merge extracted values into existing state (never overwrite with null). When state.input_type == "user_symptom" (coming from emergency_node fallback): Attempt to extract available fields from symptom description first. Only ask for fields that genuinely cannot be inferred.
node.question_generation: LLM must generate ONE question at a time (not all missing fields at once). Priority order: duration, symptom_severity, symptom_count, patient_age_risk, comorbidity_flag. Question must be conversational, empathetic, and medically appropriate. Never ask the same question twice per session. Track asked_questions in state to prevent repetition.
node.routing_logic: IF any required field is still None after answer processing: set state.pending_question = generated_question route to output_node (mode="query") IF all required fields are populated: route to classification_node

node.purpose: Final medical urgency classification. Calls external ML classification API with all 5 triage parameters. Interprets urgency_score into label. Routes to hospital_finding_node or output_node based on label.

external_api_call.error_handling: timeout=8s, retries=2. On failure use rule-based fallback: score = (symptom_severity * 0.4) + (comorbidity_flag * 0.3) + (age_risk_score * 0.3) where age_risk_score: low=0.2, medium=0.5, high=0.9

node.score_interpretation: 0.0 to 0.39 = Self-care 0.40 to 0.74 = Visit clinic 0.75 to 1.0 = Emergency At exact thresholds use the upper/more urgent label (conservative choice)
node.routing_logic: IF label == "Self-care": set state.urgency_label = "Self-care" route to output_node (mode="suggestions") IF label == "Visit clinic": set state.urgency_label = "Visit clinic" route to hospital_finding_node IF label == "Emergency": set state.urgency_label = "Emergency" route to hospital_finding_node

node.purpose: Finds nearest appropriate medical facility based on user location and urgency level. Always routes to output_node after API call (with or without results).

field.name: user_location
field.note: If None, ask user to share location in output

external_api_call.error_handling: timeout=10s, retries=1. On failure or no location: set state.hospital_data = None. Output node will handle gracefully by telling user to search manually.

node.routing_logic: Always route to output_node. mode="emergency" if urgency_label is Emergency. mode="clinic" if urgency_label is Visit clinic.

node.purpose: Final response generator. The ONLY node that produces output visible to the user. Uses LLM to generate empathetic, accurate, medically appropriate responses tailored to the output mode.

output_modes.mode: LLM generates a warm, clear follow-up question to gather missing triage data. Tone: empathetic nurse asking a patient. Include brief context for WHY the question is being asked.

LLM answers the user's healthcare query using its medical knowledge. Must include: direct answer, important caveats, when to seek help. Add disclaimer: This is informational, not a substitute for medical advice.

Urgency = Self-care. LLM generates: urgency assessment, step-by-step self-care instructions, warning signs that would require escalating to clinic or ER, estimated recovery timeline. Tone: caring GP explaining to a patient.

Urgency = Visit clinic. LLM generates: urgency assessment, why clinic visit is recommended, what to tell the doctor, nearest clinic details from hospital_data, what to do while waiting for appointment.

Urgency = Emergency. LLM generates: BOLD emergency alert, immediate action steps (call ambulance etc.), nearest emergency hospital from hospital_data with full contact details, what to do RIGHT NOW while waiting for help. Tone: urgent but calm. Never panic the user.

node.response_schema: { "output_type": "query" | "answer" | "suggestions" | "clinic" | "emergency", "urgency_label": "Self-care" | "Visit clinic" | "Emergency" | null, "message": str, "action_items": [str], "hospital_info": { ... } | null, "disclaimer": str | null, "session_complete": bool }

files_to_generate.file: Define ALL Pydantic v2 models: - GraphState (TypedDict for LangGraph state — full field list with types and defaults) - InputRequest (FastAPI request body) - OutputResponse (FastAPI response body matching output_schema above) - EmergencyAPIRequest / EmergencyAPIResponse - ClassificationAPIRequest / ClassificationAPIResponse - HospitalAPIRequest / HospitalAPIResponse - Hospital (individual hospital model) - ConversationTurn (for history tracking) - SessionData (full session object stored in memory) Every model must have field validators, examples, and descriptions.

Define ALL system prompts as module-level constants: - INPUT_CLASSIFIER_PROMPT - QNA_ANSWER_PROCESSOR_PROMPT - QNA_QUESTION_GENERATOR_PROMPT - OUTPUT_QUERY_PROMPT - OUTPUT_ANSWER_PROMPT - OUTPUT_SUGGESTIONS_PROMPT - OUTPUT_CLINIC_PROMPT - OUTPUT_EMERGENCY_PROMPT Each prompt must: - Set precise role and persona - Define exact JSON output schema with field descriptions - Include examples of good and bad outputs - Include explicit instructions for handling ambiguous inputs - Include tone and style guidelines - Include medical accuracy and safety disclaimers

Complete LangGraph StateGraph implementation: - Import all schemas, prompts, and utilities - Define async node functions (one per node) - Define all conditional edge routing functions - Build the graph: add_node, add_conditional_edges, add_edge - Compile the graph - Expose compiled graph as module-level app variable - Include session state management (get_session, update_session) - Include all external API caller functions with full error handling - Include LLM caller utility (async, with retry, with JSON parsing and validation) - Include the complete graph visualization comment at the top showing all edges

FastAPI application: - POST /chat endpoint (takes InputRequest, returns OutputResponse) - GET /session/{session_id} endpoint (returns current session state) - DELETE /session/{session_id} endpoint (clears session) - Health check endpoint - CORS middleware - Request/response logging middleware - Proper HTTP error handling

code_quality.mandate: Every function has a complete docstring with Args, Returns, Raises
Every external API call has timeout, retry, and fallback
Every LLM call has JSON validation with Pydantic and retry on parse failure
Type hints on every parameter and return value — no bare dict or Any
All prompts use f-strings with explicit variable injection — no ambiguity
Logging at DEBUG level inside nodes, INFO level for routing decisions
No TODO comments, no placeholder functions, no implement later stubs
Graph state fields default to None — never throw KeyError
Conversation history capped at last 20 turns to prevent context overflow
All PII fields handled with care — no logging of raw user_message content

edge_cases.case: User sends empty message: input_node catches, returns validation error via output_node
User describes emergency symptoms mid-query conversation: input_node re-classifies and emergency_node fires
All 5 QnA fields already provided in first message: qna_node skips questions, goes straight to classification_node
External API returns HTTP 5xx: use fallback logic, never crash the graph
LLM returns malformed JSON: retry up to 3 times with corrective prompt, then use safe fallback
User location not available: hospital_finding_node sets hospital_data=None, output_node handles gracefully
Session not found: create new session automatically
User sends "I'm having chest pain": MUST route to emergency_node immediately, never to QnA first
User asks a general query AFTER a symptom session: answer query but remind to continue symptom assessment
comorbidity_flag inference: if user mentions diabetes, heart disease, cancer, or COPD: auto-set to True
patient_age_risk inference: elderly, 60+, infant, child: extract age risk automatically
urgency_score exactly at threshold 0.4 or 0.75: use upper label (conservative, safer choice)

safety_rules.rule: NEVER tell a user they are NOT having an emergency if they describe chest pain, difficulty breathing, sudden severe headache, loss of consciousness, stroke symptoms, severe bleeding, or suicidal ideation. These ALWAYS route to emergency_node first.
Every output in answer mode must end with: If your symptoms worsen or you feel unsafe, please seek immediate medical attention or call emergency services.

Self-care suggestions must always include a list of RED FLAG symptoms that would require escalation. Never give self-care advice for symptoms that could mask serious conditions such as appendicitis, pulmonary embolism, or meningitis.
Never diagnose. Always say "this may indicate" or "these symptoms are often associated with" — never "you have [condition]".
Always recommend professional consultation in the disclaimer, regardless of urgency level.

example_flows.flow: User: "I'm having severe chest pain radiating to my left arm and I can't breathe" input_node: input_type=user_symptom, urgency_signals=[chest_pain, radiating_arm, breathing_difficulty] emergency_node: API returns is_emergency=True hospital_finding_node: finds nearest ER output_node(mode=emergency): "This is a medical emergency. Call emergency services NOW..."

User: "I have a headache and mild fever" input_node: input_type=user_symptom, no critical signals qna_node: symptom_severity=None, asks about severity output_node(mode=query): "I want to make sure I give you the right guidance. How would you rate the intensity of your headache? (1=mild, 10=excruciating)" [Next turn] User: "About a 4, it is uncomfortable but I can function" input_node: input_type=user_answer qna_node: extracts symptom_severity=0.4, asks next question about duration [After all fields collected] classification_node: score=0.25 — Self-care output_node(mode=suggestions): "Based on your symptoms, self-care should be sufficient..."
User: "What is the difference between viral and bacterial fever?" input_node: input_type=user_query output_node(mode=answer): Comprehensive medical answer with disclaimer

User: "I've had lower back pain for 2 weeks, I'm 55 with diabetes" input_node: user_symptom, comorbidity_flag auto-inferred=True, patient_age_risk=medium emergency_node: not emergency qna_node: fills remaining fields classification_node: score=0.52 — Visit clinic hospital_finding_node: finds nearest clinic output_node(mode=clinic): "You should visit a clinic soon. Here are the closest options..."

prompt.final_instruction:
Now generate ALL FOUR files completely. Start with schemas.py, then system_prompts.py, then workflow.py, then api.py. Do not stop between files. Do not summarize what you would write — write it. Every class, every function, every prompt, every edge, every handler — complete and production-ready.
The graph compiled at the end of workflow.py must be runnable with: result = await app.ainvoke(initial_state)
The FastAPI server in api.py must be runnable with: uvicorn api:app --reload
Ship it.
